title: What is the plan and how was this review conducted?

default.alt: Overview visual from chapter 1 context slide

stepVisuals.alt: Context slide showing data collection methods

steps.title: Plan foundation
steps.body: The Plan for Ageing Well 2020-2025 was designed to help older South Australians live well through three priorities: Home and Community, Meaningful Connections, and Navigating Change.

steps.title: How evidence was gathered
steps.body: The review combined a large survey, interviews, focus groups with priority groups, organisational input, and policy/media analysis to understand what is working and what is not.

steps.title: Why this chapter matters
steps.body: This chapter establishes confidence in the findings and sets up the three priority-area chapters that follow.

stepVisuals.alt: Accompanying image for Home and Community chapter
Home and Community slide with challenge statements
Home and Community chapter key takeaway

stats.label: Feel they can age in place

steps.title: What is working well
steps.body: Most older South Australians value their neighbourhoods and feel positive about remaining in their homes as they age.

steps.title: Where pressure is building
steps.body: Renters, people with disability, and regional residents face greater constraints. Housing diversity has also reduced compared with ten years ago.

steps.title: Key takeaway
steps.body: People want to stay in their communities, but affordable and adaptable housing options are limited and age-friendly planning needs stronger visibility.

stepVisuals.alt: Meaningful Connections slide with challenge statements

stats.label: Have someone they can rely on

steps.title: Strengths
steps.body: Many older people have strong support networks, and volunteering participation in South Australia remains high.

steps.title: Challenges
steps.body: Social isolation remains significant for some groups, especially when transport barriers and ageism limit participation.

steps.title: Key takeaway
steps.body: Connection is a core driver of ageing well outcomes, but transport access and age-inclusive attitudes are not yet consistent.

stepVisuals.alt: Navigating Change slide with challenge statements

stats.label: Feel services focus on individual needs
Main barriers: digital and system complexity

steps.title: What is working
steps.body: Culturally tailored services improve safety and trust for Aboriginal and Torres Strait Islander communities, and community organisations help CALD groups navigate systems.

steps.title: What is difficult
steps.body: Many older people report services are hard to navigate and insufficiently personalised, with digital access gaps compounding the problem.

steps.title: Key takeaway
steps.body: Older people want support that aligns with their priorities, but current service complexity and digital barriers reduce access and confidence.

stepVisuals.alt: Next steps slide showing four themes
QR code for statewide conversations updates

steps.title: The transition
steps.body: The review findings now feed into Statewide Conversations to shape the next ageing well plan.

steps.title: Four themes
steps.body: Wellbeing, Stronger Connections, Life Transitions, and Home and Community provide the structure for the next engagement phase.

steps.title: Call to action
steps.body: The final chapter should include your engagement prompt and QR asset so audiences can follow and contribute.
